from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about universities (or related entities) into a single
    fluent sentence. Each predicate is mapped to a short verbal phrase; the resulting
    clauses are concatenated with commas.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate → phrase mapping for natural language rendering
    phrase_map = {
        "city": "is located in the city",
        "country": "is located in the country",
        "latinName": "has the Latin name",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed",
        "rector": "has rector",
        "academicStaffSize": "has an academic staff size of",
        "established": "was established in",
        "state": "is in the state",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
        "dean": "has dean",
        "location": "is located at",
        "numberOfStudents": "has number of students",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "director": "has director",
        "motto": "has motto",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has number of postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has number of undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official school colour",
        "outlookRanking": "has outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given the technical campus status by",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "religion": "has religion",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "river": "has river",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "legislature": "has legislature",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "has neighboring municipality",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus",
        "president": "has president",
        "staff": "has staff",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has number of doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has elevation above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "longName": "has long name",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to enable richer, single‑sentence rendering per entity
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    sentences = []
    for subject, preds in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for pred, obj in preds:
            verb_phrase = phrase_map.get(pred, pred)
            # Keep original quoting; add quotes only if the object looks like a plain phrase with spaces
            if not (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) and " " in obj:
                obj = f'"{obj}"'
            parts.append(f"{verb_phrase} {obj}")

        # Build a fluent clause for this subject
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {parts[0]}"
        else:
            # commas between all but the last, and 'and' before the final part
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + f", and {parts[-1]}"
        sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into one (or more) sentences
    final_text = " ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output
    return final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
